findings.description: ReDoS / algorithmic-complexity DoS: `search()` compiles the caller-supplied `name_pattern` as a regex (re.compile(name_pattern, re.IGNORECASE)) with no validation, length cap, or timeout, then runs matcher.search() over every user's display_name; a crafted pattern (e.g. '(a+)+$' or '(.*a){40}') triggers catastrophic backtracking and pins a CPU core indefinitely, exhausting the worker pool from a single request (CWE-1333 / CWE-400 / CWE-730; OWASP A05 Injection / A06 Insecure Design).

Unbounded resource consumption: when `department` is None the query has no LIMIT and `.fetchall()` materializes the entire (non-deleted) users table into memory, builds a dict per row, and sorts in Python before any pagination is applied, so any caller can force a full-table load + full Python-side sort on every request — a memory/CPU amplification DoS (CWE-400; OWASP A06 Insecure Design).

Missing function-level access control on a state-changing operation: `deactivate(user_id)` soft-deletes ANY user by id with no caller-identity or authorization check, so if exposed as an entry point (per the stated threat model) any actor can deactivate arbitrary accounts (CWE-862 / CWE-639 / Broken Access Control, OWASP A01); confidence moderated because authz may be enforced by an out-of-scope caller.

User enumeration / information disclosure: `by_email(email)` returns a full user record (id, display_name, email, department) for any email and is an oracle for whether an address exists, enabling enumeration and profile harvesting with no rate limit or authz hook (CWE-204 / CWE-639, OWASP A01); confidence moderated because access control may live in an out-of-scope caller.

Scanner-reported Log Injection (run_audit.py:66 [medium]) appears to be a FALSE POSITIVE: the format string is a constant and all three logged arguments (len(window), total, page) are integers formatted with %d — no attacker-controlled string is interpolated, so there is no CRLF/log-forging vector (a non-int `page` would raise TypeError via %d, not inject); recorded for evidence reconciliation, not a real vulnerability.

Pagination off-by-one (deferred to logic critic): offset = page * page_size with a 1-based `page` skips the first window, making rows 0..page_size-1 unreachable on page 1; no direct security impact (negative `page` yields an empty Python slice, not an out-of-bounds data leak), noted only for cross-critic awareness.
